title: Notification for Collection of Admit Cards – Junior Engineer (Civil) Examination 2026
TEJAS District Awareness Programme Conducted at Lunglei
content:
80/2026-2027

Lunglei, 14 May 2026:The TEJAS District Awareness Programme under the Startup India initiative was successfully conducted today with 130+ participants at the Convention Centre Hall, Lunglei, through a collaboration between Startup India, Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India, Mizoram Startup Mission under the Planning & Programme Implementation Department, Government of Mizoram, and the Office of the Deputy Commissioner, Lunglei District.

The programme was graced by Pi Navneet Mann, IAS, Deputy Commissioner, Lunglei, as Chief Guest. In her special address, the Deputy Commissioner highlighted the significance of the TEJAS initiative as one of the few entrepreneurship-focused programmes specifically designed for non-metro and rural regions of the country. She stated that the initiative reflects an inclusive approach towards entrepreneurship development by creating opportunities for youth and aspiring entrepreneurs outside metropolitan cities.

The Deputy Commissioner spoke extensively on the entrepreneurial potential of Lunglei district, particularly in sectors such as natural farming, organic products, bamboo-based enterprises, banana cultivation and processing, and value-added local products. She noted that Lunglei possesses inherent talent and resources that can be transformed into sustainable economic opportunities through skill development, market linkages, and institutional support.

She further highlighted successful local entrepreneurial examples and stressed the importance of strengthening forward and backward market linkages so that locally produced goods can access wider markets within and outside the state. She also acknowledged the role of Mizoram Startup Mission and allied departments in supporting entrepreneurs through mentorship, incubation support, and market access initiatives.

During her address, the Deputy Commissioner also referred to various Government schemes and livelihood initiatives being implemented in Lunglei district, including flagship entrepreneurship and livelihood support programmes, One District One Product (ODOP), banana cluster development initiatives, skill development programmes, and SHG-based livelihood projects under rural development initiatives. She encouraged participants to actively utilise available support systems and entrepreneurial schemes to build sustainable enterprises.

The programme commenced with inaugural and welcome remarks from the DC Office, highlighting the entrepreneurial landscape and opportunities in Lunglei district. This was followed by a context-setting session by Pi Ruth Chuauthangpuii from Mizoram Startup Mission (MSUM), who provided an overview of the TEJAS initiative and its implementation in Mizoram.

Pi Vanlalparmawii, MSUM, delivered a session on Startup India and TEJAS, covering DPIIT startup recognition, benefits available under Startup India, funding opportunities, market access, and the role of startups in generating employment and driving economic growth.

Pu Samuel Vanremruata, MSUM, spoke on the startup ecosystem in Mizoram and the importance of strengthening incubation culture and institutional support systems at the district level. The session highlighted Government policies, ecosystem-building initiatives, and the need for innovation-driven entrepreneurship in Mizoram.

Following the high tea session, Pu Mark Lalduhsaka, State Lead, RAMP, Commerce & Industries Department, conducted a masterclass on Supply Chain Management and later delivered a session on One District One Product (ODOP) and MadebyMIZO, focusing on branding, value addition, and market linkage opportunities for district-specific products.

Pu Lalremsanga, GST Expert, conducted a masterclass on simple bookkeeping practices for entrepreneurs along with an overview of GST and basic financial compliance.

A session on the Mizoram BanaKaih Venture Capital Fund was delivered by Pu Lalngaihawma, MSUM, who explained the objectives of the fund, eligibility criteria, application procedures, and funding support available for startups in Mizoram.

The programme concluded with a vote of thanks from the DC Office, followed by a networking and tea session among participants, speakers, and stakeholders.

The TEJAS initiative is being implemented across selected districts in India to strengthen entrepreneurship ecosystems in non-metro and underserved regions by creating awareness on startup support systems, government schemes, innovation pathways, mentorship, and funding opportunities.

title: Lungleiah TEJAS District Awareness Programme buatsaih a ni
content:
537/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭomir thla ni 14:Startup India, Department for Promotion of Industry and Internal Trade (DPIIT), Ministry of Commerce & Industry, Government of India te, Mizoram Startup Mission (MSUM), Planning & Programme Implementation Department, Government of Mizoram leh Lunglei Deputy Commissioner te ṭang ho huaihawtin TEJAS (Transforming Entrepreneurial Ecosystem in Aspirational Districts for Jobs & Self-Employement) District Awareness Programme chu vawiin khan Lunglei Convention Centre Hall-ah hlawhtling taka buatsaih niin, sumdawnna kalpui mek leh bul ṭan tum te, he lama tui mi leh zirlai mi 130 chuang an tel.

He hun hi Lunglei District Bawrhsap Pi Navneet Mann chuan Khuallian niin a hmanpui a. TEJAS chu khawpui tenau zawk leh thingtlanga chengte tana sumdawnna bul ṭanna kawnga puitu ruahmanna ṭha tak a nih thu sawiin, ṭhalaite leh a tui mite tana ṭanpuina leh kaihhruaina ṭha tak awm chu District ṭhalaite'n chhawr ṭangkai se a duh thu a sawi a. Sumdawnna kawnga bul ṭan nan Lunglei District-ah hma lak theihna tam tak a awm thu sawiin, damdawi hmang lova thlai chin te, mau hmanga thil siam chhuah te, balhla chin leh sawngbawl te, ei tur chi hrang hrang sawngbawl kawngah te hma lak theihna tam tak a awm thu a sawi a. Mipuite pawhin chu lam atan chuan thiamna leh theihna an neih ṭhat thu sawiin, kaihhruaina ṭha an dawn chuan sang tak thleng thei tur leh hlawhtling thei tur tam tak an lo chhuah a beisei thu a sawi a. He lam kawnga sawrkarin hma lakna hrang hrang a neihte chu chhawr ṭangkai tum turin ṭhalaite leh a tui mite a chah.

He hun hi Pi Carolyn Lalduhawmi'n kaihruaiin, inlawmna thu a sawi a. MSUM aṭangin Pi Ruth Chuauthangpuii chuan TEJAS chungchang leh Mizorama kalpui a nih dan a sawi fiah a. Pi Vanlalparmawii (MSUM) chuan Startup India leh TEJAS hnuaia hamṭhatna awmte leh hma lak dan tur hrang hrang a zirtir a. Pu Samuel Vanremruata (MSUM) chuan Mizorama sumdawnna bul ṭan tharte dinhmun leh sawrkar hma lakna pawimawhna te a tar lang bawk.

Chawhnu lamah Commerce & Industries Department hnuaia RAMP State Lead Pu Mark Lalduhsaka chuan sumdawnna kalpui kawnga Supply Chain Management pawimawhna leh One District One Product (ODOP) leh MadebyMIZO chungchang a zirtir a. GST Expert Pu Lalremsanga chuan bungrua leh sum che vel chhinchhiah dan te, GST chhiah leh sum enkawl chungchang te a zirtir a. Pu Lalngaihawma (MSUM) chuan Mizoram BanaKaih Venture Capital Fund chungchang leh a dil theih dan te a sawi fiah a. Lunglei SDC Pu Lalruatfela'n lawm thu sawiin hun a khar.

TEJAS initiative hi India ram chhunga district thlan bikahte kalpui niin, khawpui tenau leh thingtlang hmun kilkhawr zawka mite'n sumdawnna bul ṭan dan kawng leh sawrkar aṭanga ruahmanna leh ṭanpuina awm theite an hriat a, an chhawr ṭangkai theihna tura inzirtirna, inkaihhruaina leh kawng inkawhhmuhna atana ruahman a ni.

title: School Education Minister Dr Vanlalthlana hovin Lungleiah "Meet Your Minister" hun buatsaih a ni
content:
535/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭomir thla ni 14:Vawiin khan Mizoram sawrkara School Education Minister Dr Vanlalthlana hovin School Education Conference Hall, Lungleiah zirlaite'n Minister hmu a, an duh zawng an zawh a, an sawipui theihna, "Meet Your Minister" hun buatsaih a ni a. Lunglei khawpui chhunga zirna in 14 aṭangin zirtirtute leh zirlaite an tel.

Education Minister Dr Vanlalthlana chuan "Meet Your Minister" hun a ṭum 4-na atana buatsaih theih a ni leh ta chu lawmawm a tih thu sawiin, zirlaite zinga hun hman chu nuam a tih thu a sawi a. Eng anga lehkhathiam leh nihna sang nei pawh mi dang tana malsawmna an nih loh chuan eng mah an ni lo va, mi dangte tana luang chhuak tam apiangin ngaih hlut an hlawh tih sawiin, khawtlang tan leh mi dangte tana luang chhuak ṭhin turin zirlaite a fuih a. An laka hmuh let leh tur beisei kan neih lohnate chunga kan chet danin mihring nihna dik tak a tar lang tih sawiin, zirlaite chu mite chunga ṭawngka leh chet ziaa ṭhat chhuah tlat turin a fuih a. Zirlaite chuan an hma hunah duhthlanna siamna tur tam tak an la hmachhawn dawn tih sawiin, duhthlanna dik siam thei turin tawn hriat nei tawhte rawn a, inchhiar zau va, inzir peih turin a fuih.

Lungleia "Meet Your Minister" hun buatsaih hi District Education Officer Pu Micky R. Lalenkawla'n a kaihruai a. Baptist Church of Mizoram (BCM) Education Department Director Rev. RC Lalthanzama'n hunserh leh zirlaite tana ṭawngṭaisakna hun a hmang a. Lunglei District chhung sikul aṭanga High School Leaving Certificate (HSLC), All India Secondary School Examination (AISSE), Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) leh All India Senior School Certificate Examination (AISSCE)-a ti ṭha zual Top 3 zirlaite lawmpuina hlan a ni bawk.

Hemi hnu hian zirlaite chuan Minister Dr Vanlalthlana chu Mizoram sawrkar hma lakna leh zirna kaihhnawiha an hriat duh hrang hrangte zawtin, an zawhnate chu Minister-in a chhan bakah, sawi hona hun hlawkthlak tak hman a ni a. Minister hian lehkhabu pahnih – "Zirna Lamtluang: Road Map to Higher Education" leh "Nung Ngam Tura Huaisenna" chu sikul telte hnenah a hlan nghal bawk.

title: RD&AD Minister Prof. Lalnilawma'n Lungsenah Community Managed Training Centre & Gender Resource Centre thar a hawng
content:
534/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭomir thla ni 14:Vawiin khan Mizoram sawrkara Rural Development & Administration Department (RD&AD) Minister Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Lunglei District-a a hmasa ber leh Mizorama a pathumna tur, Community Managed Training Centre (CMTC) & Gender Resource Centre (GRC), Lungsen a hawng.

CMTC Conference Hall, Lungsena a hawnna inkhawm buatsaihah Khuallian Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Self Help Group-in a tum pakhat chu mahni inrin tawkna neih a nih thu sawiin, CMTC hawn thar chuan SHG nuho chu an inring tawk tawh hle tih a lan chiantir thu a sawi a. Hmeichhiate chu chhungkaw tana mi pawimawh leh khai dingtu ber an ni chho tawh tih sawiin, sawrkar pawhin an chanvo a dah pawimawhin, hmasawnna kawng a dapsak reng tih a sawi a. CMTC chu a hawn tawhnaah ṭangkai taka hman a nih thu sawiin, CMTC Lungsen pawh chu ṭangkai taka hman a nih a beisei thu a sawi a. Lungsen RD Block tan bik SHG dawr 6 sakna tur tih fel a nih tawh thu leh national highway pal tlang a nih avangin phalna hmuh hun hunah sak nghal a nih tur thu sawiin, Lungsen leh a chhehvel chu khualzinte tlawh tam hmun (tourist site)-a siam turin hma lak chhoh mek a nih thu leh chumi hun atan chuan khualzinte tan pawh CMTC hawn thar chu ṭangkai hle tura beisei a nih thu a sawi.

MzSRLM Lungsen Block hnuaia Thorang–I Cluster Level Federation enkawl tur Community Managed Training Centre & Gender Resource Centre Lungsen hawnna inkhawm hi Lungsen Block Development Officer Pu Lalhruailiana Zote chuan a kaihruai a. MzSRLM Chief Executive Officer Pi Zosangzuali Pachuau chuan technical report pein, Lungsen RD Block-ah hian kum 2019-ah MzSRLM a luh thu leh tun dinhmunah thawktu mi 10 awmin, Self Help Group 224, Village Organisation (VO) 20 leh Cluster Level Federation (CLF) 3 a awm thu a tar lang a. SHG member 79,833 aṭangin kum khata cheng nuai 1 la lut tling 'Lakhpati Didi' 350 an awm tih a sawi bawk.

He hunah hian RD&AD Additional Secretary Pu V. Lalengmawia, ZPM General Headquarters Vice President Pu C. Lalnunnema leh Thorang–I CLF President Pi Zoramthangi te'n thu an sawi bawk a. MzSRLM Lungsen Block Mission Manager Pi Zothanpuii Hmar chuan lawm thu sawiin hun a khar.

Vawiina hawn tak CMTC Lungsen hi 79ftx26ft-a zau a ni a. A building sak nan leh a chhunga thil dah tur lei nan cheng nuai 43.5 sen niin, Gender Resource Centre siam nan hian a hranpain cheng nuai 5 dawn a ni bawk a. Pindan pali – Gender Resource Centre, Training Hall, Kitchen cum Dining Hall leh riahna (Dormitory with Bathrooms) a awm a. Mi 26 an riak lut thei dawn a ni.

title: 3-Day Training of Private School Teachers in Aizawl on School Health Module
content:
532/2026-2027

Aizawl, 14.05.2026:MSACS leh SCERT tangkawpin Aizawl-a Private School zirtirtute puala School-a hriselna lam hawi zirtirna training “School Health Module” chungchanga ni thum awh training (May ni 12 a tan) a buatsaih, SCERT Auditorium, Aizawl a neih chu vawiin khan a kharna hun neih a ni.

National Health Mission hnuaia programme pakhat - School Health Programme chuan Mizoram chhunga Sawrkar school zirtirtute chu School Health Module hmangin training an lo pe tawh thin a. tun tumah hian MSACS leh SCERT tawngkawp chuan Mizorama school naupang tam thei ang berin he zirtirna tha tak mai an dawn theih nan Private school zirtirtute chu training an pe thung a ni.

Training hawnna inkhawm May ni 12 khan School-a hriselna lam hawi zirtirna training hawnna inkhawm chu SCERT Auditorium-ah neih a lo ni tawh a, MSACS Project Director, Dr. Jane Rinsangi Ralte chuan thu sawiin, zirtirtute chu naupangte ngaihsan (role model) an nih angin an thu kamchhuakte chu naupangten an ngai pawimawh thin a, chuvangin training-a an thil zirte chu an zirlaite hnenah tha taka zirtirturin a chah bawk. Training hawnna inkhawmah hian Dr. Jane Ralte chuan kan ram HIV dinhmun tarlangin, Kum 1990 atanga kumin March 2026 thlenga kan rama HIV kai pun dan te thlirho a ni a, MSACS in HIV do nana a hmalakna hrang hrangte tarlangin, he natna do tur hian Sawrkarte, pawl hrang hrangte leh mipuite tanho a tulzia a sawi.

Kum 2024 leh 2025 chhung khan Mizoram a private school 552 te chu training pek an lo ni tawha, MSACS leh SCERT te chuan tun tumah hian private school training tura koh atanga School zirtirtu 50 zet an in ko khawm thei chu lawmawm an ti hle a ni.

Training module-ah hian topic sawm pakhat (11) a awm a, chungte chu - Growing up Healthy, Emotional Wellbeing and Mental Health, Interpersonal Relationships, Values and Responsible Citizenship, Gender Equality, Nutrition Health and Sanitation, Prevention and Management of Substance Misuse, Promotion of Healthy Lifestyle, Reproductive health and HIV Prevention, Safety and Security Against Violence and Injuries, Promotion of Safe use of Internet and Social Media Behaviour te a ni a. Zirtirtu kalkhawmte chu heng topic hrang hrangte hi zirtir an ni.

Training kharna inkhawm hi Pu PC. Vanlalruata, Deputy Director, SCERT hova neih a ni a, zirtirtute chu Participation Certificate hlan an ni.

title: HOME MINISTER PU K. SAPDANGA’N PTS, THENZAWLAH INFRASTRUCTURE THAR A HAWNG
content:
531//2026-2027

Serchhip, the 14th May, 2024:Vawiin khan Home Minister Pu K. Sapdanga’n Police Training School, Thenzawlah hmasawnna ruhrel thar - PTS gate pui siam thar te, digital lab, indoor gymnasium leh Indoor Firearms Training Simulator te a hawng. Hawnna inkhawmah hian Mizoram DGP Pu Sharad Agarwal, IPS leh Mizoram Police-a senior officer thenkhat leh department dang atangin officer eng emaw zat an tel a ni.

Home Minister Pu K. Sapdanga chuan Police Department in “Year of Discipline” programme thar a kalpui chu hêng hmalakna tharte hian nasa taka a tanpui a beisei thu a sawi a. Police hnaah discipline, professional conduct leh physical fitness te a pawimawhzia sawiin PTS-a training standard a tihsan lehzual tur thu a sawi bawk. 70th Batch Recruit Constable trainees-te lakah pawh beiseina sang tak a neih thu sawiin, recruitment process dik tak leh langtlang taka kalpui an nih ang ngeia Mizoram tana police force professional leh thiam tak an nih zel a beisei thu a sawi bawk.

• PTS Main Gate: PTS Main Gate thar hi sum awm remchang ang ang hmanga sak niin, mi dang ruai lova PTS-a thawkte leh training laite’n an sak liau liau a nih vangin sum tlemte sengin tha takin siam a ni a.

• Digital Laboratory: Police training tih tunlai (modernize) zelna atan Digital Lab., computer set 20 leng neih thar a ni bawk a. Hei hian computer zirna tihchangtlun, digital learning leh ICT-enabled training atan nasa taka a puih beisei a ni.

• Firearms Training Simulator: Police training laite’n dik tak leh him zawka silai khawih leh kah an zir theihna tur Firearms Training Simulator thar pawh din a ni a. He Simulator-ah hian AK-47, INSAS, Glock pistol leh silai dangte a rualin a kah theih a ni.

• Gymnasium: Taksa tihchaka vawn hrisel nan Gymnasium siam thar siam a ni bawk a. Building awmsa siam that niin gym bungraw thar changkang tak tak dah thar a ni a, Police training-te leh staff-te tan a tangkai hle tura ngaih a ni.

Heng hmasawnna thar hi Mizoram Home Minister leh DGP te hmalaknain, Assistance to States & UTs for Modernisation of Police (ASUMP) Scheme hmangtein a a hun tak tihhlawhtlin an ni a. Heng hmalakna thar hrang hrangte hian Police Training School-in police training tih changtlun leh tih hmasawnna a ngaih pawimawhzia a tilang bawk a ni.

title: KHAWZAWL DDMA THUKHAWM

title: CVSc&AH Selesih Organises Press Conference on Promoting Sustainable Agriculture Practices
content:
79/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 14th May 2026:The College of Veterinary Sciences & Animal Husbandry (CVSc&AH), Central Agricultural University (CAU), Selesih, today organised a Press Conference for media persons to highlight the importance of adopting sustainable agricultural practices that reduce reliance on chemical fertilisers and pesticides. The event, held at the Conference Hall of CVSc&AH, Selesih, was themed “Promoting Alternative Agriculture Practices and Enhancing Nutrient Management Efficiency.”

Dr. Lalnuntluangi Hmar, Dean of CVSc&AH, CAU, Selesih, addressed the gathering and underscored the critical need for achieving self-sufficiency in food production. She emphasised the vital role of the media in creating widespread awareness about scientifically validated and environmentally friendly farming methods. Acknowledging the multiple challenges confronting the agricultural sector, Dr. Lalnuntluangi Hmar pointed to declining soil fertility, rising costs of agricultural inputs, the impacts of climate change and the long-term harmful effects of excessive chemical fertiliser use. She stressed the need to move beyond a narrow focus on increasing yield and instead adopt farming practices that ensure long-term soil health, environmental sustainability and improved economic returns for farmers.

She noted that sustainable farming practices, which minimise soil degradation, can significantly reduce production costs while increasing farmers’ net income. In this context, she highlighted that India is actively encouraging a shift towards organic and alternative farming systems with reduced dependence on chemical inputs. The Dean informed the media that the three Central Agricultural University institutions in Mizoram - CVSc&AH Selesih, College of Horticulture Thenzawl and KVK Aizawl - are actively implementing various initiatives to promote alternative agriculture. These include farmer training programmes, Kisan Gosthis, field demonstrations and the extensive use of social media to reach farmers across the state.

She informed the media that the College of Horticulture, Thenzawl, has been producing and distributing Liquid Biofertilizers to farmers. The three institutions are committed to delivering the latest scientific knowledge and technologies to the farming community. Today’s press conference was organised as part of these ongoing efforts, she added. Dr. Lalnuntluangi Hmar appealed to all stakeholders to adopt improved, safe and more profitable farming practices and to extend full support to farmers during this transition. She also assured that experts from Central Agricultural University are always available to assist farmers facing any agricultural challenges.

title: CEO Dr. Lalrozama’n Mizoramah Special Intensive Revision (SIR) neih a ni dawn tih a puang
content:
527/2026-2027

14th May, 2026:Vawiin khan Election Commission of India chuan a bik taka electoral roll ennawnna Special Intensive Revision (SIR) thawh thumna neih a nih tur thu a puang a. He thawhthumnaah hian Mizoram tiamin State 16 leh Union Territory 3-ah neih a ni dawn. Vawiin chawhnu dar 3 khan Aizawl Press Club-ah Press Conference kovin Dr. Lalrozama, Chief Electoral Officer chuan Mizoramah SIR neih tur a nih tur thu a puang a ni.

Dr Lalrozama chuan Mizorama Intensive Revision neih hnuhnung ber chu kum 2005 ah niin, kum 20 a liam hnuah 'Special Intensive Revision' neih a nih tur thu a puang a. Hei hi kalphung pangngaia Electoral Roll ennawn thin ang ni loin, “Tun tuma Election Commission of India in ti tura min tih hi chu a dan a dang a. Electoral Roll revision hi a aia thuk zawk leh changtlung zawka neih tur a ni a, BLO ten Enumeration Form nen in (household) tin an tlawh dawn a, Roll thar hlak siam tur a nih avangin voter zawng zawng ten kan ngaih pawimawh hle a tul a ni,” a ti.

Election Commission of India-in SIR thawhthumna a puan rual hian Mizoramah pawh hmalak tan nghal a ni ta a. Kar tharah hian District tina DEO ten Enumeration Form an print anga, hei hi ni 3 chhunga peihfel tura beisei an ni. He Enumeration Form nen hian May 30 - June 28, 2026 chhungin BLO te chu in (household) tinah vawi thum aia tlem lo an kal dawn a. Hemi hun chhung hian in lama biak theiha lo awm hram turin CEO chuan mipuite a ngen a. BLO te hian an bial theuha in tinah Enumeration Form hi an sem anga, in neitute hnenah thil pawimawh an hrilhfiah anga, dahkhah (fill up) tur te an zawh hnuah BLO te vek hian an khawn khawm leh vek ang a. Ennumeration Form submit lo te chu draft roll-ah an chuang dawn lo a ni. Enumeration Form hi copy 2 sem a ni dawn a, pakhat chu voter ta tur niin BLO in a sign hnan anga, pakhat zawk erawh BLO ta tur a ni thung ang.

SIR ah hian kum 2005-a Mizorama Intensive Revision lo neih tawha Electoral Roll leh kum 2025 electoral roll chu enmil a ni dawn a. BLO te chuan kum 2005 Roll-a awm tawh leh la awm lote en dikin, he 2005 Electoral Roll-a chuangte chu an fate leh an thlahte awm leh awm loh nen an enmil hnuah thlunzawm (map) a ni a. Kum 2005 E Roll-ah hian voter 5,59,123 awmin kum 2025 E-Roll ah hian voter 8,75,004 an awm thung a. 2005 leh 2025 E-Roll enmila, percentage of self and progeny (E Roll a hming chuang te leh an thlahte) mapping tihhlawhtlin zat chu 80.37% a ni.

Langtlang leh rintlaka E Roll siam a nih theih nan Political Party tin te chu Block Level Agent (BLA) inruat tura tih an ni a. AAP hian BLA an la inruat lo a; BJP -294, INC-1045, MNF-868 leh ZPM -1223, an vaiin BLA 3430 ruat an ni tawh a ni.

Special Intensive Revision ah hian July 1, 2026 chu qualifying date ah hman tur niin July 1, 2026 a kum tling hman te Roll ah thun an ni dawn a. May 30 - June 28, 2026 chhungin BLO ten in tin an tlawh anga. Draft Electoral Roll hi July 5, 2026 ah tlangzarh a ni anga. Tlangzarh a nih veleh CEO website, ERO office, Village Council leh Local Council office ah tarchhuah a ni dawn a, a duh apiang tan en theih a ni ang. Lungawilohna nei ten claims & objection an thehluh hun hi July 5 atanga August 4, 2026 a ni a. Heng claims and objections chinfel hun chhung hi July ni 5 atanga September 2, 2026 a ni a. Final Electoral Roll hi September 6, 2026 ah tlangzarh a ni ang.

CEO Dr Lalrozama chuan mobile app -ECINET download turin mi tinte a ngen a. He app hmang hian kum 2005 Intensive Revision ah voter ten an hming a chuan leh chuan loh an en thei a. Hei bakah hian BLO phone theihna te, complain thehluhna te, application thlen chin chhuina (track)-na te leh thil dang tam tak atan a chhawr tangkai theih a ni.

Vawiin Press Conference ah hian CEO bakah Joint CEO Pi Ethel Rothangpuii, Deputy CEO Pu James Miahlung leh SIR Media Nodal Officer Pi Lalnunmawii te an tel a ni.

title: CVSc& AH Selesihah awmze nei zawka Agriculture kalpui dan tur chungchangah Press Conference neih a ni
content:
526/2026-2027

Aizawl, the 14th May, 2026:College of Veterinary Sciences & Animal Husbandry (CVSc&AH), Central Agriculture University (CAU), Selesih chuan vawiin khan thuthar thehdarhtute tan chemical fertilizer leh rannung thahna hlo a innghat lova, daih rei zawk leh leilung tichhe lo zawnga Lo neih chawilar tulzia hrilhhriatna Press Conference, “Promoting Alternative Agriculture Practices leh Enhancing Nutrient Management Efficiency” tih thupui hmangin Conference Hall, CVSc&AH, Selesihah a buatsaih.

Dr. Lalnuntluangi Hmar, Dean, CVSc&AH, CAU, Selesih chuan thupui thlan chu ei leh bara intodelh tura pawimawh tak a nih thu sawiin, Lo neih dan nghet leh tlo bakah environment humhalh pawimawhnate mipui zirtir kawngah media-te nena thawhhona tha neih a tul thu a sawi a. Agriculture-in harsatna tam tak a tawh chhoh mek zel thu sawiin, lei da, thlai thar chhuah nana senso sang chho zel, sik leh sa inthlak leh chemical fertilizer nasa taka hman ten nghawng tha lo tak a thlen thu a sawi a. Thar chhuah tihpun ringawt tinzawn lova, daih rei zawk leh leilung hriselna humhalh chunga kuthnathawktute tana hlawk zawka thar thlai chhuah dan zawnpui a tul thu sawiin, hemi a nih avang hian India ram pumah chemical-a innghat lova organic uar lehzual tura hmalak a ni, a ti a. Daih rei zawk leh leilung tichhe lo zawnga Lo neih chuan sum sen a tih hniam thu leh sum lakluh pawh a tihpun thu a sawi bawk.

Mizoram chhunga Central Agricultural University hnuaia institution pathum - CVSc&AH, Selesih; College of Horticulture, Thenzawl leh KVK, Aizawl-te chuan alternative agriculture lama hmasawn nan loneitute zirtirna hrang hrang, Kisan Gosthi, Field Demonstration leh social media hmangtein Mizoram hmun hrang hrangah an kalpui thu a sawi a. College of Horticulture, Thenzawlin liquid biofertilizer siam chhuakin loneitute hnena a pek chhuah thu a sawi bawk a. Heng institution pathumte hian tunlai thiamnain mitin hnen a thlen theih nan hma lak zel an tum thu sawiin, press conference chu hemi peng pakhat a nih thu a sawi a. Lo neih dan tha, him leh hlawk zawk kalpui a nih theih nan kuthnathawktute chu ngaihtuahna lo seng tura chahin, thawhhona tha tak neih puiah a sawm a. Harsatna neite chuan Central Agricultural University hnuaia mithiamte an rawn theih reng thu a sawi bawk.

title: MINISTRY OF DoNER IMMEDIATELY SANCTIONS FUNDS FOR GINGER PROCESSING UNIT
content:
78/2026-2027

Aizawl, 14th May 2026:Yesterday evening, Chief Minister Shri Lalduhoma and DoNER Minister Shri Jyotiraditya Scindia jointly inaugurated the Mizoram Ginger Unique Selling Proposition (USP) Project through virtual mode. The project, with an outlay of Rs. 189.78 crore, is titled Sustainable Ginger Cultivation & Value Chain Development Project.

Under the project, components such as Sustainable Cultivation, Farm Mechanization and other related activities will be implemented in convergence with other Ministries. The Post-Harvest Management & Processing component, however, is to be implemented through funding under the NEC Focused Development Component (FDC). An amount of approximately Rs. 30.13 crore has been earmarked for this purpose.

Immediately after the project inauguration programme yesterday, the Ministry of DoNER formally sanctioned the amount of Rs. 30.13 crore through the North Eastern Council (NEC) for implementation of this component.

As stated earlier, under the project plan, the main processing hub will be established at Mualkawi in Champhai District. In addition, smaller spoke units will be set up at Tualcheng, Vaphai and Zotlang to support the main facility.

The required preparatory and developmental works are planned to be undertaken during the current year, with the ginger processing facilities expected to become operational from next year for value addition and processing of ginger produce.

Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma expressed his gratitude to the Minister of DoNER for the prompt sanction of funds.

title: RD&AD Minister Prof. Lalnilawma'n RGSA hnuaiah Lunglei District chhunga Village Council 22 te hnenah Computer Set leh Printer a sem
content:
522/2026-2027

Lunglei, 2026 Ṭomir thla ni 14:Rural Development & Administration Department (RD&AD) Minister Prof. Lalnilawma chuan tukin khan Lunglei Circuit House Conference Hall-ah Rashtriya Gram Swaraj Abhiyan (RGSA) hnuai aṭangin Lunglei District chhunga Village Council 22 te hnenah Computer Set leh Printer a hlan.

Prof. Lalnilawma chuan Rural Local Body-te'n sawrkar laipui duhsakna chang a, hmasawnna hna thawhna tur an dawng ṭhin chu a nihlawhthlak hle tih sawiin, fund tang tam tak a zavaia dil chhuah theih a ni chu sawrkar hotute, thawktute leh VC-te thawh hona vang a nih thu a sawi a. VC aṭangin computer set dilna an dawn nual tawh thu sawiin, tuna computer set inhlan chu khawtlang tan ṭangkai taka hmang tur leh uluk taka enkawl turin VC-te a chah a. VC-te'n Own Source Revenue (OSR) atan VC Act thar hmangin property tax leh animal registration tax te an khawn ṭan tawh tih sawiin, he'ng fund te hi khawtlang tana ṭangkai thei ang bera hmang turin a chah a. He'ng OSR an khawn dan azir hian 16th Finance Commission hnuaiah phei chuan Performance Grant te a awm dawn tih sawiin, VC-te chu ṭhahnem ngai leh theih tawp chhuah turin a ngen a. OSR tling khawm aṭanga khawtlang mamawh hna an thawh, fund-in a daih loh a awm chuan sawrkarah thlen tura chahin, a theih anga ṭanpui an inhuam thu a sawi a. VC-te chu sawrkar hnuaia committee pawimawh hrang hrangah hotu chan chang an nih angin dik leh lang tlang taka rorel tur te, party bil sawi lova khawtlang hmasawn nana ṭang ho zel tur te'n a chah.

RGSA hnuaia Computer Set leh Printer semna inkhawm hi Lunglei District Local Administration Officer Pu Beitlotha Nohro chuan a kaihruai a. RD&AD Additional Secretary Pu V. Lalengmawia chuan Minister kaihhruaina hnuaiah thawktute'n Rural Local Body-te hmasawn nan theih tawp an chhuah tih sawiin, 15th FC fund chu State sawrkarin dawng tlai deuh mah se, a hun taka hman zawh theih a ni tur chu lawmawm a tih thu a sawi a. Ni za inhlawhna (VB G RAM G)-ah sawrkar laipuiin thla lak dan thar a ruahman tawh avangin dik leh felfai takin demand siam ṭhin ni se ṭha a tih thu sawiin, computer set an dawn tharte pawh khawtlang tana hna thawh nan ṭangkai taka hmang turin a chah.

Local Administration Department (LAD) Director Dr Lalngura Tlau chuan RGSA chungchang leh LAD hma lakna tlangpui sawi fiahin, RGSA hnuaiah Computer Set sem bakah, VC-te tan capacity building programme te, Panchayat Bhavan sakna leh exposure visit te kalpui ṭhin a nih thu a sawi a. Sawrkarin VC-te tihchangtlun nan theih tawpin hma a la mek tih sawiin, 16th Finance Commission chu rei lo teah VC-te'n an thawk leh thuai dawn a, chumi atan chuan VC tinte'n mahni Own Source Revenue (OSR) tun aia ṭhaa lak luh dan ngaituah a ṭul tih a sawi a. Panchayat Advancement Index (PAI) 2.0 chu tlangzarh mai theih a nih thu sawiin, Mizoram chhunga VC-te PAI 2.0-a an tih ṭhat dan State-wise leh District-wise-a tar lan a nih tur thu a sawi a. Kawrthah North VC te chuan India ram pum puiah Clean and Green Panchayat thematic-ah pakhatna an nih avangin RGSA aṭangin cheng vaibelchhe 1 an dawng tawh tih tar langin, VC dangte paw'n dawng ve thei tura theih tawp chhuah turin a chah.

All Mizoram Village Council Association (AMVCA) Secretary Pu K. Lalzarliana leh ZPM (General Headquarters) Vice President Pu C. Lalnunnema te pawhin thu an sawi a. LAD Joint Director Pu ST Lalhmingmawia'n lawm thu sawiin hun a khar.

title: MINISTRY OF DONER IN SAWHTHING PROCESSING UNIT DINNA TUR SANCTION NGHAL
content:
520/2026-2027

Aizawl the 14th May 2026:Nimin tlai lamah khan Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma leh DoNER Minister Pu Jyotiraditya Scindia te chuan online-in Mizoram Ginger Unique Selling Proposition Project, Rs. 189.78 Crore senna tur - Sustainable Ginger Cultivation & Value Chain Development Project an hawng a.

He project-a Sustainable Cultivation, Farm Mechanization leh a dangte hi Ministry dangte nena thawkho (Convergence) a kalpui tur a ni a, Post-Harvest Management & Processing erawh hi chu NEC (Focussed Development Component) Fund atanga thawh tur a tih a ni a. A thawh nan Rs. 30.13 Crore chheh hran a ni.

Nimina Project hawnna inkhawm neih zawh veleh khan Ministry of DoNER chuan he sum Rs. 30.13 Crore hi NEC ah hian a sanction fel nghal a ni.

Kan lo tarlan tawh ang khan ruahman danah chuan a process-na hmunpui ber (Main Hub) hi Mualkawi, Champhai District-ah din a ni ang a. A chawmtu turin a chhehvel khaw pathum Tualcheng, Vaphai leh Zotlangah unit tenau zawk (Spoke hub) din a ni ang. Kuminah hnathawh tur tul zawng zawng thawh zawk a, nakkum Sawhthing tharchhuah sawngbawl hman tum a ni.

Rang taka pawisa an rawn sanction avang hian Chief Minister Pu Lalduhoma chuan Minister of DoNER chungah lawmthu a sawi a ni.

title: Minister Pi Lalrinpuii'n MSU leh MCAB buatsaih HSSLC-a tithate lawmpuina hun a hmanpui
content:
519/2026-2027

Dated Aizawl: the 14th May 2026:Vawiin khan Health & Family Welfare Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan Vanapa Hall-ah, Mizoram Board of School Education-in kumin 2026 Higher Secondary School Leaving Certificate (HSSLC) result a tihchhuah tak a Science, Arts leh Commerce stream-a Mizo zirlai tithate chawimawina hun MSU leh Mizoram Cooperative Apex Banks Pvt. ltd. buatsaih chu khuallian niin a hmanpui a. MCAB Chairman Pu Lalnunsanga Rokhum chu khualzahawm a ni.

Khuallian Minister Pi Lalrinpuii chuan thusawiin, Zirlai tithate hlawhtlinna chu kum tluana an thawhrim rah a nih thu sawiin, thil tha tih reng reng a thlawn ngailo a ti a. Mizoramin Result tha tak tak a neih thin chu lawmawm tiin, Toppers-te leh zirlai kalkhawmte chu inthlahdah mai lo tura fuihin, sang tak an thlen tawhna chu a aia sang zawk thlen tum turin a chah a ni. Minister chuan hlawhtlinna kawng zawh tura pawimawh tak tak Discipline leh Time Management chu mahni nunah theuh seng lut turin kalkhawmte chu a chah a. Zirlaite chu bul an tan chauh a ni tih inhriain, huaisenna leh taimakna nen bei chhunzawm zel tura chahin, “ Tumkhat hlawhtlin hi a tawk mai lo a, vawikhat hlawhchham pawh a in siamthat theih, a pawimawh ber zawk erawh chu bei chhunzawm zel tura huaisenna neih”, a nih thu a sawi bawk. Minister chuan Mizo-te All India Service leh Central Service-ah te inziak tling kan awm tlem ta chu a ngaimawh thu sawiin, kalkhawmte chu Mizoram chhung mai nilo, India ram inelna sang zawk UPSC-ah te leh NEET-ah te inziak tling thei tura tan la sauh sauh turin a fuih bawk a ni. Zirna kan ngaih pawimawh tluk zet hian hriselna kan dahsan a pawimawh thu sawi bawkin, zirlaite leh kalkhawmte chu natna ruihhlo leh a kaihhnawih mipat hmeichhiatna thianghlimlo te lakah fihlim tur leh, nun invawng tak nei turin a fuih bawk a ni.

Khualzahawm Pu Lalnunsanga Rokhum pawhin thu sawiin, zirlaite chu ram leh hnam tana chhenfakawm tak nih tum theuh turin a chah a,zirna mai bakah entrepreneurship-ah te an rilru seng turin a fuih bawk a ni. Zirlaite chu Zoram hmasawnna rahbi an nih thu sawin, ram hmel timawitu ni thei turin hmalam pan zel turin a chah a, an kal zelna turah duhsakna a hlan nghal bawk. MSU President, Pu B. Lalkhawngaihzuala pawhin fuihna thu sawiin, zirlaite chu chawimawina dawng pha ve lo an nih pawhin beidawng mai loin, khawvel intlansiakna chu a intan chauh a ni tih hriain, bei zel turin a chah bawk a ni.

He hunah hian zirlaite tana pawimawh tak career guidance chungchang inzirtirna neih niin, Resource person Dr. Lalhruaitluanga , Zo-Career founder chuan hun a hmang a ni.

Ziralai titha chawimawina hlante chu:

1.Science stream:1st Rank – Lalmoipuia Lawmzual, St. Pauls HSS2nd Rank - K.Zomuanpuia, BHSS Lunglei2nd Rank - LH Lalrammuanpuii, St. Joseph HSS3rd Rank - Zothanhlira, St. Pauls HSS

2. Commerce stream:1st Rank - Vanlaltleipuia Chhangte, St. Pauls HSS2nd Rank - Rothangpuia, St. Joseph HSS4th Rank - Lydia Sui Nei, Oikos HSS

3. Art stream:1st Rank – Lalnunhlui Sailo, St. Pauls HSS2nd Rank - Willis Chawngthanseia, St. Pauls HSS3rd Rank - Vanlalngaihzuali Sailo, Helen Lowry HSS

Vawiina hun hmannaah hian MSU hruaitute leh Zirlai tithate school an tel a. Hun hi  Pu Lalrampanmawia, Treasurer, MSU-in kaihruaiin, Pu Lalruatpuia, General Secretary, MSU chuan  MSU hmalakna hrang hrangte report a pe a. He hunah hian khuallian, khualzahawm leh MSU president-tein Zirlai tithate hi chawimawina an hlan a ni.

title: MLA Er. H Ginzalala chuan Vaikhawtlangah Solar Dryer leh MHIP Rûn a hawng
content:
517/2026-2027

Champhai, dated 14th May 2026:Champhai North bialtu MLA Er. H Ginzalala chuan Vaikhawtlangah Solar Dryer, Kuthnathawktu ten an Baibing tharchhuah te awlsam zawka Ni zung chakna hmanga an rêp ro zung zung na tur chu a hawng a, Ruahmanna an lo siam angin heng an baibing rêp ro hralhchhuah tur atana a hming an vuah "ZO-BAIBING' tih chu a tlangzarh nghal bawk. He khawl hi a hmangtu tur kuthnathawktu te hnenah hman theih turin a hlan nghal a ni.

He hun hi Vaikhawtlang Community Hall ah hman a ni a. Pu Thangliansinga chuan kaihruaiin MLA chuan Solar Dryer chu khaw hmasawnna thlentu ni thei se a duh thu a sawi a, Vaikhawtlanga Baibing chingtu te chuan an tharchhuah hmanga Zoram Khawvelah hmingthanpui turin a beisei a. Mizoram chhung mai ni lo, ram dang thlenga thawnchhuak thei khawpa ṭan la turin he hmalakna hi kalpui a ni a, chumi atan chuan theihtawp chhuah turin kut hnathawktute chu a chah bawk a ni. Sum leh pai senso tam lutuk lo khawvelin a ken tel chakna hmanga hmasawnna kalpui a ṭul hle a ni tih a sawi a, thildangah pawh ngaihtuahna a sen reng thu a sawi bawk a ni.

Hemi hnu hian MLA Fund hmanga Vaikhawtlang MHIP Rûn sak chu hawnna a nei nghal a, MHIP hruaitu te leh khawtlang hruaitu te an lawm hle a, Serthlum chingtu te Lo kalpawhna tur Kawng sial (Link road) enfiahna hun a hmang nghal bawk.

MLA hi Pu James Lalnithanga, Project Director, DRDO bakah Loneitu te leh Khawtlang hruaitu te'n an ṭawiawm a ni.

title: MP (LOK SABHA)  SHRI  RICHARD VANLALHMANGAIHA CHAIRS LAWNGTLAI DISTRICT DEVELOPMENT CO-ORDINATION & MONITORING COMMITTEE (DISHA) MEETING
content:
77/2026-2027

Lawngtlai, 14th May 2026:The Lawngtlai District Development Co-ordination and Monitoring Committee (DISHA) meeting was held today at the DC Conference Hall under the chairmanship of Shri Richard Vanlalhmangaiha, Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha).

Shri Richard Vanlalhmangaiha expressed gratitude to all the members attending the meeting. He emphasized the importance of sincere and effective implementation of various Centrally Sponsored Schemes within the stipulated time. Highlighting the crucial role of Line Departments, he urged officials to continue working dedicatedly for the welfare and development of the people and the district. He also stated that timely conduct of DISHA meetings is highly beneficial and appreciated the preparedness and commendable work carried out by different departments.

The MP further stressed that successful implementation of Central Government schemes in Lawngtlai District is essential for public welfare, district development, self-reliance in food production, and overall progress. He encouraged all departments to continue giving their best efforts in carrying forward developmental activities. Acknowledging that difficulties may arise during the implementation of schemes, he urged departments not to hesitate in approaching him so that possible solutions could be explored collectively.

The DISHA meeting was attended by Lawngtlai East MLA Dr. Lorrain Lalpekliana Chinzah, Adviser to the Chief Minister (H&FW, A&FW), Deputy Commissioner & Member Secretary Shri Donny Lalruatsanga, officials from various line departments, representatives from LADC and CADC, NGOs, and members of the Mizoram Journalists’ Association (MJA).

During the meeting, members reviewed the progress of various Centrally Sponsored Schemes implemented in Lawngtlai District during 2025–2026. Discussions were also held regarding challenges faced during implementation, possible solutions, future course of action, and a question-and-answer session was conducted.

The meeting discussed several schemes and programmes including RKVY, SHC, PM-KISAN and other schemes under the Agriculture & Farmers Welfare Department; PMEGP under Commerce & Industries Department; Samagra Shiksha, PM-POSHAN (Mid-Day Meal) and PM SHRI Schools under School Education Department; PMMSY under Fisheries Department; National Food Security Act and Price Monitoring under Food, Civil Supplies & Consumer Affairs Department; various programmes under the National Health Mission (NHM); initiatives of Lead Bank and SBI; PMGSY and PARIVAHAN under PWD; JJM, SBM(G) and Urban Water Supply Schemes under PHE Department; initiatives under Power & Electricity Department; MGNREGS, PMAY-G, PMKSY 2.0, MzSRLM, DDU-GKY and RSETI under RD&A Department; NSAP under Social Welfare Department; various initiatives under Sports & Youth Services Department; AMRUT 2.0, PMAY- HFA(Urban) 2.0, and SBM(U) under UD&PA Department; ICDS, BBBP, PMMVY, Saksham & Poshan 2.0 and Mission Shakti under Women & Child Development Department; and DILRMP, SVAMITVA initiatives under CADC.

DISHA is a programme under the Ministry of Rural Development, Government of India, aimed at monitoring Centrally Sponsored Schemes across districts and strengthening coordination among departments implementing Central Government schemes. The Member of Parliament (Lok Sabha) serves as the Chairman, the Member of Parliament (Rajya Sabha) as Co-Chairman, while the Deputy Commissioner serves as the Member Secretary.
